questions.question: The unprecedented ______ of the disease left the healthcare system paralyzed.

options.A: proliferation
options.B: eradication
options.C: stagnation
options.D: mitigation
options.E: vindication

questions.question: Despite the ______ evidence presented by the prosecution, the jury remained unconvinced.

options.A: irrefutable
options.B: ambiguous
options.C: negligible
options.D: superficial
options.E: tangential

questions.question: The researcher's ______ approach to data collection ensured no variables were overlooked.

options.A: meticulous
options.B: haphazard
options.C: sporadic
options.D: lethargic
options.E: transient

questions.question: Although quantum computing promises to revolutionize cryptography, ______ .

options.A: it currently faces significant physical limitations
options.B: it has already broken all existing encryption methods
options.C: there is no theoretical basis for its functioning
options.D: classical computers are inherently faster
options.E: the field was abandoned a decade ago

questions.question: ______ , the empire's economic infrastructure was already crumbling.

options.A: Long before the barbarian invasions began
options.B: Because the trade routes were highly secure
options.C: While the military expanded its territories effortlessly
options.D: If the emperor had implemented the new tax laws
options.E: Since agricultural output reached unprecedented heights

questions.question: Sociologists argue that social media creates echo chambers ______ .

options.A: whereby individuals are only exposed to affirming viewpoints
options.B: unless algorithms prioritize diverse news sources
options.C: even though face-to-face interactions are increasing
options.D: as if digital communication were completely obsolete
options.E: so that critical thinking can be universally fostered

questions.question: ______ establishing a permanent base on the Moon, space agencies must solve the problem of radiation exposure.

options.A: Prior to
options.B: In spite of
options.C: Similar to
options.D: In contrast with
options.E: As a result of

questions.question: The newly discovered fossil provides a crucial link in mammalian evolution; ______ , it raises new questions about their geographical origins.

options.A: however
options.B: moreover
options.C: consequently
options.D: for instance
options.E: in other words

questions.question: The Industrial Revolution (1) ______ a major turning point in Earth's ecology.

options.A: marked
options.B: has marked
options.C: had been marking
options.D: was marked
options.E: would mark

questions.question: Human populations grew exponentially, (2) ______ massive deforestation.

options.A: leading to
options.B: resulting from
options.C: bringing about by
options.D: stemming from
options.E: consisting of

questions.question: Factories emitted unprecedented levels of carbon dioxide, (3) ______ the natural greenhouse effect.

options.A: exacerbating
options.B: mitigating
options.C: alleviating
options.D: ignoring
options.E: preserving

questions.question: (4) ______ some early scientists warned about pollution, their concerns were largely ignored.

options.A: Although
options.B: Because
options.C: Since
options.D: As if
options.E: Just as

questions.question: Today, we are still dealing with the environmental legacy (5) ______ that era.

options.A: of
options.B: for
options.C: with
options.D: by
options.E: on

questions.question: Küresel ısınma, sadece kutup ayılarını değil, tüm deniz ekosistemlerini tehdit etmektedir.

options.A: Global warming threatens not only polar bears but all marine ecosystems.
options.B: Global warming is a threat to polar bears as well as marine ecosystems.
options.C: Not only polar bears but also all marine ecosystems are threatened by global warming.
options.D: Marine ecosystems and polar bears are both threatened by global warming.
options.E: Global warming threatens all marine ecosystems, especially polar bears.

questions.question: Yeni araştırmalar, uyku eksikliğinin bilişsel işlevler üzerinde kalıcı hasar bırakabileceğini gösteriyor.

options.A: New research indicates that a lack of sleep can leave permanent damage on cognitive functions.
options.B: Recent studies show that sleeping less damages cognitive functions permanently.
options.C: New research has shown that cognitive functions are permanently damaged by lack of sleep.
options.D: According to new research, lack of sleep might cause permanent damage to cognitive functions.
options.E: New studies suggest that a lack of sleep causes permanent damage on cognitive functions.

questions.question: Yapay zeka etiği, teknolojinin gelişim hızına ayak uydurmakta zorlanmaktadır.

options.A: The ethics of artificial intelligence is struggling to keep pace with the rapid development of technology.
options.B: Artificial intelligence ethics has difficulty keeping up with the speed of technological development.
options.C: The speed of technological development makes it hard for artificial intelligence ethics to keep up.
options.D: Artificial intelligence ethics is struggling to match the development speed of technology.
options.E: Keeping up with the rapid development of technology is a struggle for artificial intelligence ethics.

questions.question: Bir dilin yok olması, sadece kelimelerin değil, koca bir kültürel mirasın yitirilmesi demektir.

options.A: The extinction of a language means the loss of not just words, but an entire cultural heritage.
options.B: When a language dies, not only words but a whole cultural heritage is lost.
options.C: The loss of a language implies losing an entire cultural heritage, not just words.
options.D: The disappearance of a language means losing a whole cultural heritage along with its words.
options.E: Extinction of a language means not only the loss of words but also of an entire cultural heritage.

questions.question: Ekonomik krizler genellikle gelir eşitsizliğini derinleştirir ve yoksul kesimleri daha fazla etkiler.

options.A: Economic crises generally deepen income inequality and affect the poor segments more.
options.B: Economic crises often deepen income inequality, affecting poorer segments more heavily.
options.C: Income inequality is generally deepened by economic crises, which affect the poor more.
options.D: Economic crises usually worsen income inequality and have a greater impact on the poor.
options.E: Generally, economic crises deepen income inequality and affect poor communities the most.

questions.question: The team could have finished the project on time if they had been provided with adequate funding.

options.A: Adequate funding would have enabled the team to complete the project on schedule.
options.B: The team finished the project on time because they received adequate funding.
options.C: Even with adequate funding, the team failed to finish the project on time.
options.D: The project was finished on time although the team lacked adequate funding.
options.E: Without adequate funding, the team still managed to complete the project on time.

questions.question: No sooner had the central bank announced the interest rate hike than the stock market plummeted.

options.A: Immediately after the central bank declared an increase in interest rates, the stock market crashed.
options.B: The stock market crashed before the central bank could announce the interest rate hike.
options.C: The central bank delayed the interest rate hike because the stock market had plummeted.
options.D: The stock market plummeted, causing the central bank to announce an interest rate hike.
options.E: As soon as the stock market crashed, the central bank announced an interest rate hike.

questions.question: Despite the complexity of the theory, the professor explained it in a way that everyone could understand.

options.A: The professor made the complex theory accessible to all through a clear explanation.
options.B: The theory was so complex that the professor's explanation confused everyone.
options.C: Because the theory was simple, everyone understood the professor's explanation.
options.D: The professor's explanation was as complex as the theory itself, confusing everyone.
options.E: Although the explanation was simple, the theory remained too complex for everyone.

questions.question: It is widely believed that renewable energy is the only viable solution to climate change.

options.A: Most people consider renewable energy to be the sole practical remedy for climate change.
options.B: Few people think that renewable energy can solve the problem of climate change.
options.C: Climate change is widely believed to be the only reason to use renewable energy.
options.D: Renewable energy is thought to be one of many viable solutions to climate change.
options.E: It is undeniable that climate change is the main obstacle to renewable energy.

questions.question: Had the symptoms been detected earlier, the patient's chances of survival would have been significantly higher.

options.A: An earlier diagnosis would have greatly increased the patient's likelihood of living.
options.B: The patient survived because the symptoms were detected early enough.
options.C: Even with early detection, the patient's chances of survival were very low.
options.D: The symptoms were detected early, yet the patient's survival chances did not improve.
options.E: The patient's survival chances were high regardless of when the symptoms were detected.

questions.question:
Dr. Smith: The new clinical trial results are quite promising. 
Dr. Jones: ______ 
Dr. Smith: Yes, especially in patients who showed no response to conventional therapies.

options.A: Did you notice any significant reduction in the tumor sizes?
options.B: Are you referring to the side effects observed in the control group?
options.C: I think we should discontinue the trial immediately.
options.D: Have they published the methodology in a peer-reviewed journal yet?
options.E: Conventional therapies are still the most reliable option.

questions.question:
Student: I'm having trouble understanding the concept of entropy in this system. 
Professor: ______ 
Student: Oh, so it's a measure of disorder or randomness rather than just heat loss.

options.A: Think of it as the degree of uncertainty or chaos in a closed system.
options.B: Entropy is essentially the amount of usable energy in the universe.
options.C: You should probably read the first chapter again before asking.
options.D: It has nothing to do with thermodynamics, strictly speaking.
options.E: Heat loss is the only important factor you need to calculate here.

questions.question:
Manager: Our quarterly profits have dropped by 15%. We need to cut costs. 
Analyst: ______ 
Manager: That might be true, but we need an immediate solution to satisfy the shareholders.

options.A: Perhaps we should look into increasing our marketing budget to drive sales.
options.B: Cutting costs now could compromise our long-term R&D projects.
options.C: I agree, laying off staff is the only logical step at this point.
options.D: The shareholders are completely unaware of this financial dip.
options.E: We have already maximized our profit margins for this year.

questions.question:
Researcher: The archaeological dig revealed artifacts from a previously unknown civilization. 
Colleague: ______ 
Researcher: Carbon dating places them around 4000 BCE, much older than the surrounding settlements.

options.A: How old are these findings estimated to be?
options.B: Where exactly did you conduct the excavation?
options.C: Were there any precious metals among the items found?
options.D: Why did this civilization collapse so suddenly?
options.E: I doubt these artifacts are authentic.

questions.question:
Policy Maker: We must implement stricter emission regulations immediately. 
Industry Rep: ______ 
Policy Maker: While I understand your financial concerns, public health must take precedence.

options.A: Such regulations will drastically increase production costs and lead to job losses.
options.B: We fully support your initiative and will comply immediately.
options.C: Public health has always been our industry's primary concern.
options.D: There is no scientific evidence linking our emissions to health issues.
options.E: We should expand our operations overseas to avoid these rules.

questions.question: Photosynthesis is the process by which green plants transform light energy into chemical energy. ______ . These sugars then serve as the fundamental energy source for almost all other organisms on Earth, directly or indirectly. Without this process, life as we know it would cease to exist.

options.A: During this process, carbon dioxide and water are converted into oxygen and energy-rich sugars
options.B: Plants also require specific soil nutrients to grow effectively
options.C: The rate of photosynthesis decreases significantly during the winter months
options.D: Cellular respiration is the opposite process, occurring in animal cells
options.E: Artificial photosynthesis is a promising new technology for renewable energy

questions.question: The Renaissance was a fervent period of European cultural, artistic, political, and economic 'rebirth' following the Middle Ages. ______ . Thinkers, authors, statesmen, scientists, and artists in Europe thrived during this era, while global exploration opened up new lands and cultures to European commerce.

options.A: It generally took place from the 14th century to the 17th century, promoting the rediscovery of classical philosophy and art
options.B: The period was marked by devastating plagues that decimated the population
options.C: Gothic architecture reached its absolute peak right before this era began
options.D: Most historians argue that the Renaissance had little impact on scientific development
options.E: The focus shifted entirely away from humanism towards strict religious dogma

questions.question: Behavioral economics combines elements of economics and psychology to understand how and why people behave the way they do in the real world. Unlike classical economics, which assumes humans are perfectly rational actors. ______ . It explains why individuals often make choices that are detrimental to their long-term financial health.

options.A: Behavioral economics recognizes that humans are prone to cognitive biases and emotional decision-making
options.B: Classical economics has completely replaced behavioral theories in modern universities
options.C: The stock market operates strictly on mathematical models ignoring human behavior
options.D: Psychology has proven to be irrelevant when analyzing macroeconomic trends
options.E: Consumers always maximize their utility by calculating all possible outcomes

questions.question: Volcanic eruptions can have a profound impact on global climate. When a large eruption occurs, massive amounts of sulfur dioxide gas are injected into the stratosphere. ______ . This aerosol layer reflects incoming solar radiation back into space, leading to a temporary cooling of the Earth's surface.

options.A: There, the gas reacts with water vapor to form highly reflective sulfate aerosols
options.B: The lava flows destroy local ecosystems, causing immediate environmental damage
options.C: Earthquakes frequently precede these massive volcanic events
options.D: Volcanoes are primarily located along tectonic plate boundaries
options.E: The carbon dioxide released contributes to immediate and severe global warming

questions.question: In literature, the concept of the 'unreliable narrator' forces readers to question the truthfulness of the story being told. ______ . By doing so, the author creates a layer of psychological complexity, requiring the audience to read between the lines to uncover the actual reality of the fictional world.

options.A: This narrator may be deceptive, naive, or mentally unstable, thus skewing the narrative
options.B: First-person narration is always the most objective way to tell a story
options.C: Omniscient narrators provide a completely unbiased view of all characters' thoughts
options.D: The protagonist is usually completely honest with the reader in classic novels
options.E: Historical fiction relies heavily on strict factual accuracy rather than narrative style

questions.question: The rapid advancement of artificial intelligence has sparked both immense optimism and profound anxiety among tech experts.

options.A: Tech experts are experiencing feelings of both great hope and deep fear due to the swift progress of AI.
options.B: AI has developed so quickly that tech experts are now mostly anxious rather than optimistic about the future.
options.C: The optimism regarding AI's rapid advancement is slowly being overshadowed by profound anxiety among experts.
options.D: Tech experts believe that the swift progress of AI will eventually resolve the profound anxiety it currently causes.
options.E: Despite the rapid advancement of AI, tech experts remain profoundly optimistic and show little anxiety.

questions.question: Unless significant measures are taken to curb deforestation, numerous indigenous species will face imminent extinction.

options.A: If substantial actions are not implemented to stop the clearing of forests, many native species will die out very soon.
options.B: Significant measures to curb deforestation have saved numerous indigenous species from imminent extinction.
options.C: Deforestation has already caused the extinction of many native species, despite significant measures being taken.
options.D: To prevent native species from going extinct, we must take minor steps to reduce deforestation.
options.E: Numerous indigenous species will survive only if they migrate away from areas experiencing heavy deforestation.

questions.question: The treaty was deemed invalid because it had been signed under duress by the defeated nation's representatives.

options.A: The agreement was considered null and void as the delegates of the conquered country were forced to sign it.
options.B: The defeated nation's representatives signed the treaty willingly, but it was still declared invalid later.
options.C: Because the treaty was signed in secret, the representatives of the defeated nation deemed it invalid.
options.D: The conquerors forced the defeated nation to sign the treaty, making it the most valid document in history.
options.E: The representatives of the defeated nation invalidated the treaty because they disagreed with its harsh terms.

questions.question: While the initial cost of installing solar panels is high, the long-term savings on energy bills make it a worthwhile investment.

options.A: Although putting in solar panels requires a large upfront expense, it pays off over time through reduced electricity costs.
options.B: The long-term savings from solar panels rarely justify the incredibly high initial costs of installation.
options.C: Because energy bills are so high, the initial cost of installing solar panels is no longer considered expensive.
options.D: Installing solar panels is a worthwhile investment only if the initial costs are subsidized by the government.
options.E: The high cost of solar panels makes them a poor investment despite the potential savings on future energy bills.

questions.question: (I) The invention of the printing press by Johannes Gutenberg in the 15th century revolutionized the spread of information. (II) Before this invention, books were meticulously copied by hand, making them rare and expensive. (III) Digital media has largely replaced physical books in the 21st century. (IV) The printing press allowed for the mass production of texts, making literature accessible to a wider audience. (V) Consequently, literacy rates across Europe surged, fueling the Renaissance and the Scientific Revolution.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V

questions.question: (I) Sleep is essential for the consolidation of memories and overall cognitive function. (II) During the REM stage of sleep, the brain is highly active, and dreaming typically occurs. (III) Many people suffer from insomnia, which can be treated with cognitive behavioral therapy. (IV) In contrast, deep sleep stages are crucial for physical repair and immune system strengthening. (V) Without cycling through these distinct sleep stages, the human body cannot maintain optimal health.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V

questions.question: (I) Cryptocurrency relies on blockchain technology to ensure decentralized and secure transactions. (II) A blockchain is essentially a distributed ledger that records all transactions across a network of computers. (III) Traditional banks are heavily regulated by government entities to prevent fraud and money laundering. (IV) Once a block of data is added to this ledger, it cannot be altered without the consensus of the entire network. (V) This immutability is what makes blockchain networks highly resistant to hacking and fraud.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V
